decisions.decision: Treat this step as additive direct-test work. Keep the existing root service-index account-route assertion, exposure-route `POST` `405` check, never-traded exposure `404` check, and successful route-vs-getter coverage instead of regrouping them into a new routing block.
alternatives_rejected: Rewrite the surrounding direct-routing tests into one new exposure-routing section before adding coverage.
Treat the task as requiring broad test reorganization instead of closing the remaining regression gap.

decisions.decision: Create the risk-only account through an accepted `POST /v1/markets/{id}/orders/probability-edit` fixture so the regression proves the public write path populates `/risk` without creating `/exposure` state.
alternatives_rejected: Seed `ACCOUNT_RISK` directly for a smaller but less representative routing fixture.
Use an EventTrade-based setup that would also create exposure state and blur the target contract.

decisions.decision: Make the new regression prove projection-local account existence by asserting the same risk-only account returns `200` from `/v1/accounts/{id}/risk` and `404 account_not_found` from `/v1/accounts/{id}/exposure`.
alternatives_rejected: Assert only the exposure-side `404` and leave the account-distinction implicit.
Treat any account with risk state as globally existing for `/exposure`.

decisions.decision: Keep this step limited to the two named empty-account variants, never-traded and risk-only, and leave zero-row or post-resolution-pruned exposure empties to existing getter tests and later read-after-write coverage.
alternatives_rejected: Add a redundant zero-row or fully pruned route-level `404` case in this step.
Expand this routing slice to cover later exposure lifecycle cleanup scenarios.

decisions.decision: Use the existing `test_get_account_exposure_serializes_lexically_sorted_positions_and_account_timestamp` route-vs-getter parity assertion as the GET-dispatch proof for this step instead of adding a second delegate-only routing test.
alternatives_rejected: Add another small `route_request("GET", ...)` delegation test that duplicates the current parity proof.
Move the parity assertion out of the exposure-projection class solely for co-location.

steps.task: Use the existing direct assertions in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` as the baseline routing seam, preserving the current root service-index account-route list, exposure-route `405`, never-traded exposure `404`, and successful GET parity coverage unless a tiny readability cleanup is needed alongside the new regression.

Add a direct backend regression in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` that creates a risk-only account through an accepted ProbabilityEdit, then asserts `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/risk` succeeds while `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/exposure` raises structured `404 account_not_found` for the same account.

Run the focused direct test slice covering account routing and exposure projection parity so the existing service-index, GET dispatch, `405`, and never-traded assertions stay green and the new risk-only `404` regression closes the remaining gap without broadening the step.
